text.title: Conversion Configuration and UTM Standards
text.summary: Conversion designation in GA4 and ad platforms, UTM parameter schema, naming conventions, and Consent Mode v2 requirements.

rule.statement: Implement Consent Mode v2 from day one even outside the EEA; retrofitting it later requires a migration and risks 30-50% conversion loss in reports during the transition.
rule.rationale: Consent Mode v2 became mandatory in the EEA in March 2024. Sites outside EEA will need to add it when expanding. Installing it at launch eliminates a future migration and ensures modeled conversion data flows correctly.

list.item: GA4 Admin → Events → mark key events as conversions
Set conversion value for revenue-generating events
Import GA4 conversion actions into Google Ads (Tools → Conversions → Import from GA4)
Set attribution window per conversion type (match to sales cycle)

rule.statement: Standardize UTM naming in code as exported constants; never rely on humans typing utm_source values manually — typos produce fragmented source/medium data that cannot be cleaned retroactively.
rule.rationale: A constant export (`UTM_SOURCES = {"google": "google", "meta": "meta"}`) prevents "Google", "google", "goog" all appearing as separate sources. Fragmented data corrupts channel attribution permanently.

list.item: utm_source: traffic origin (google, meta, linkedin, twitter, email, affiliate) — lowercase only
utm_medium: marketing channel type (cpc, paid_social, email, organic_social, referral)
utm_content: ad variation format [type]_[description]_[version] e.g. video_testimonial_v2

antipattern.description: Using spaces or uppercase letters in UTM values.
antipattern.reason: Spaces get encoded as %20 or + differently across browsers and tools. Mixed case creates separate entries in GA4 reports. Both produce reporting fragmentation that requires manual cleanup.

rule.statement: Set analytics_storage and ad_storage to "denied" by default before the consent banner renders; only upgrade to "granted" after explicit user consent is recorded.
rule.rationale: Collecting analytics data before consent is recorded violates GDPR in the EEA and is increasingly enforced. Denied-by-default is the only approach that survives a regulatory audit.

rule.statement: Hash all PII (email, phone) via SHA-256 on lowercase-trimmed values before sending to ad pixels; use server-side tagging to prevent raw PII from reaching client-side code.
rule.rationale: Ad platforms require hashed PII for enhanced matching. Sending raw PII violates GDPR and ad platform terms. Skipping normalization (lowercase + trim) produces near-zero match rates.
